tournament.name: KikOpen III - Torneos 4 Región de Coquimbo

decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo, which involves playing the Dreadnought along with Stifle to counter the triggered ability and have a powerful 12/12 creature in play for only one mana. The deck also includes various counterspells and draw spells to protect the combo and dig for the necessary cards. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently executing the combo to overwhelm them with a huge threat.

The deck is focused on quickly and aggressively dealing damage to the opponent using cards such as Lightning Bolt, Fireblast, and Pyrostatic Pillar. It includes creatures like Grim Lavamancer and Mogg Fanatic to maintain pressure on the opponent. The goal is to control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot while utilizing burn spells and efficient creatures to finish the game quickly. The deck also includes cards like Black Vise and Sulfuric Vortex to further punish the opponent and restrict their options.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively deal damage to the opponent by using creatures like Ball Lightning, Grim Lavamancer, Jackal Pup, and Mogg Fanatic, as well as burn spells like Incinerate, Lightning Bolt, Fireblast, and Price of Progress. The deck also includes efficient card advantage with Cursed Scroll and Urza's Bauble. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, unrelenting damage while leveraging the synergy between burn spells, creatures, and aggressive mana base to win the game swiftly.

This deck focuses on using efficient creatures and removal spells to control the game, while also having a toolbox aspect with cards like Living Wish to find specific answers. The Rock archetype prioritizes grinding out resources and disrupting the opponent's game plan, using cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress to strip away key cards from their hand. With a mix of powerful threats and versatile removal, this deck aims to outlast and outvalue the opponent, while also having the ability to recur key creatures with cards like Recurring Nightmare.

The deck focuses on using Hermit Druid to mill your whole deck, putting creatures like Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Triskelion in the graveyard. Then, use combinations like Phyrexian Devourer and Triskelion to deal massive damage to your opponent. The deck also utilizes cards like Survival of the Fittest and Cabal Therapy to set up your combos efficiently. By carefully managing your resources and graveyard, you can pull off powerful combos and overwhelm your opponent quickly. The key to success lies in effectively setting up your graveyard while disrupting your opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of aggressive goblin creatures that can deal damage efficiently and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey to cheat creatures into play, Goblin Matron to search for key cards, and Goblin Ringleader to draw more threats, the deck aims to establish board presence early and maintain pressure throughout the game. With added support from cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander, the deck can quickly close out games before opponents have a chance to stabilize.

This Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy, while putting pressure on them with efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade. The deck also includes removal spells such as Snuff Out and Diabolic Edict to deal with opposing threats. The inclusion of cards like Graveborn Muse and Cursed Scroll helps to maintain card advantage and provide additional ways to close out the game quickly. With a mix of hand disruption, creature aggression, and removal, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The Mono Black Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through discard spells like Duress and controlling the board with removal spells such as Diabolic Edict and Smother. The deck also utilizes The Rack and Bottomless Pit to apply pressure and drain the opponent's life total. With Mishra's Factory and Wasteland providing additional utility and disruption, the deck aims to outlast and outvalue the opponent in a slow and methodical manner.

This deck focuses on flooding the board with aggressive Goblin creatures and using their tribal synergy to overwhelm the opponent quickly. Utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to get key pieces onto the battlefield as quickly as possible, the deck aims to deal damage fast and efficiently. By maximizing the tribal bonuses provided by cards like Goblin Warchief and Goblin King, along with removal options like Gempalm Incinerator, the deck can create a fast clock for the opponent while also disrupting their game plan with cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures with the Madness ability, allowing for efficient casting of spells from the graveyard. The key strategy revolves around quickly overwhelming the opponent with a mix of powerful creatures like Arrogant Wurm and efficient beaters like Savannah Lions. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Cursed Scroll and Seal of Cleansing to control the board. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy with a mix of lands that provide colored mana and utility. Overall, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent and disrupt their game plan.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through countermagic, removal spells, and board wipes while using cards like Standstill to generate card advantage. It also aims to lock down the opponent's resources with cards like Dust Bowl and Seal of Cleansing. The win condition is typically through Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave while efficiently managing resources with land destruction and card draw. This deck excels in stalling the game until it can establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm the opponent.

The main focus of this Mono Black Aggro deck is to quickly deploy aggressive creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade while disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress. The deck also aims to control the board with removal spells like Smother and Chainer's Edict, while utilizing efficient mana acceleration with Dark Ritual. The deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of fast creatures, hand disruption, and removal spells while maintaining a solid mana base with Mishra's Factory and Wasteland.

The main concept of the deck is to utilize creatures with strong abilities or recursion effects in combination with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes Survival of the Fittest as a key card to search for specific creatures in the deck for various situations. The mana base consists of a mix of Forests, Swamps, Llanowar Wastes, and Gaea's Cradle to support the color requirements of the cards in the deck.

The deck focuses on assembling the Phyrexian Dreadnought combo as quickly as possible while using spells like Stifle, Counterspell, and Foil to protect it. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan through counterspells and card draw while also controlling the pace of the game. The inclusion of cards like Vision Charm and Peek help to filter through the deck and find key combo pieces. Overall, the deck thrives on efficiency and timing to execute its win condition effectively.

The focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful threat that is hard to deal with. By using disruption spells like Meddling Mage and Counterspell to protect the combo, while also drawing cards and controlling the opponent's plays with cards like Accumulated Knowledge and Portent, this deck aims to win the game through a quick and unexpected victory. The versatile utility of cards like Peek and Vision Charm, along with efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares, support the deck's game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle to create a powerful win condition. By playing cards like Counterspell, Foil, and Gush to control the game and protect the combo, the deck aims to quickly assemble its pieces and overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of card draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Impulse help to find the combo pieces more efficiently. Overall, the strategy is to disrupt the opponent while setting up the game-winning combo as fast as possible.
